title: Miner's horror

The '''miner's horror''' was a ancient large, space-borne creature of mysterious origins [[Vacuumbreather|found only]] in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Often over twenty meters in size, they were vaguely fish-shaped, with large dorsal spines and a set of large, saw-like teeth. They fed on [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]], which they chewed into dust and slowly digested and refined into the pure metals and minerals which nourished them. Over decades, a miner's horror could eat entire asteroid belts.<ref name="CG" /> 

As they were constantly hungry, they were a significant hazard to inhabited asteroids, [[Space station/Legends|space stations]], and [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]], which they would devour for their metallic content. Luckily for most [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]], miner's horrors were fairly easy to outrun. If they could not be outrun, however, they were difficult to destroy. All the same, mining companies sometimes sponsored hunting expeditions in order to recover refined ore from a horror's body.<ref name="CG" /> 

It was uncertain where the miner's horrors originated, with the first recorded miner's horror was encountered by a group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in the galactic fringes circa [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. Their reports were not believed, until the remains of a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] attacked by one of the beasts was discovered. Two armed [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] went after the creature, with only one returning intact.<ref name="CG" /> 

Since then, rare sightings of miner's horrors continued, though the most sightings in the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]] were in the relatively remote [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]]. These were all sightings of lone creatures, save one sighting of three horrors eating the wreckage of a recent battle. Some believed that the miner's horrors were part of a very slow-moving invasion force, and that larger miner's horrors than the freighter-sized creatures encountered to date were on the way.<ref name="CG" />
